items.headline: MCP security got tighter in Claude Code
items.body: the 2.1.196 release bundles enhanced MCP security alongside background session reliability fixes and a leaner /code-review workflow. worth a pass over ADO MCP Server to see if any of its auth or transport handling was working around gaps this closes. if the changelog is vague on specifics, that's a reason to check the diff directly, not skip it.

items.headline: sonnet 5's tokenizer quietly raises costs
items.body: sonnet 5 is now the default model in claude code as of 2.1.197, and per simon willison it matches opus-level output at a lower sticker price but tokenizes less efficiently, pushing real costs up roughly 30%. that default-model swap directly affects this portfolio's own scout and reviewer agents, which run on whatever claude code ships by default. not a crisis given the subscription model, but worth watching if turn budgets start feeling tighter.
